reasoning: Exact matches on name+artist were too restrictive and name fields differ slightly (e.g., inclusion of artist in title on one side). Semantic similarity over name and artist will capture fuzzy title variations and formatting differences while keeping candidate pairs manageable.

Previous exact-match multi-column blocking was too strict (0.23 vs 0.8). Album/title strings differ slightly between datasets (e.g., artist prefixed in name), so embedding-based similarity on name and artist will capture fuzzy matches while keeping the block size manageable.
